title: Canonical Project Model — shared definitions
description: Reusable building blocks referenced by every section schema via relative $ref (e.g. "common.defs.schema.json#/$defs/provenance"). Keeping these in one place is what makes the model 'common' across all projects: every value is wrapped the same way, every reference is shaped the same way. Tools that do not resolve $ref can still read the data — the shapes are documented here.

provenance.description: Where a value came from. Inherited from the Mistral OCR4 extraction envelope so any downstream tool can cite and re-verify. Carried on every normalized record.

source_doc.description: doc_id from the project-intake handoff (e.g. doc002-rfp).

page.description: Zero-based page index in the source document.

bbox.description: [x0, y0, x1, y1] bounding box on the page, when the extraction provided one.

confidence.description: Extraction confidence for this value, when OCR4 reported one. Low values land in needs_human_review.

extracted_verbatim.description: The raw text exactly as it appeared in the document, before any normalization. The single source of truth for the value.

normalization.description: How a verbatim value was transformed into its canonical form. Present only when the structurer changed something (unit conversion, CSI mapping, entity merge). Makes every derived value auditable; empty means the value is used as written.

actions.description: What was done, e.g. 'uom: SY->SF', 'csi_mapped: 09 51 00', 'deduped: merged 2 contacts', 'split: lump sum allocated across SOV lines'.

by.description: Section normalizer agent / rule that produced this value.

money.description: A monetary value as carried in the model. amount is the normalized number; as_written preserves the source string. The model never invents a price — amount is null unless a value was present in a document.

basis.description: Pricing basis as stated in the source.

quantity.description: A measured or stated quantity. value + uom are the normalized pair; as_written preserves the source.

uom.description: Normalized unit of measure: EA, LF, SF, SY, CY, CF, TON, LB, GAL, LS, HR, DAY, MO, etc.

waste_factor.description: Allowance/waste multiplier as a fraction (0.05 = 5%) when stated. Null = none applied.

csi_ref.description: Classification anchor. CSI MasterFormat (most recent version, divisions 01-32 primary) is the ONLY classification system in this model. masterformat_division is the universal sort and grouping key — every trade, line item, cost code, submittal, quick link, and critical-path item carries one, and documents/pages/tables/sheets are sorted by it. See resources/masterformat-divisions.json for the division list.

masterformat_division.description: Two-digit MasterFormat division, e.g. '09' (Finishes), '26' (Electrical). REQUIRED — the universal sort/grouping key. Use '01' for general requirements when no better fit; never leave blank.

masterformat_section.description: Full MasterFormat section number, e.g. '09 51 00' (Acoustical Ceilings).

company_cost_code.description: The firm's internal cost code mapped from the MasterFormat code, when known (e.g. a demo vs. new-work split within a division).

party_ref.description: A pointer to an entry in the subcontractors/contacts directory, by stable id. Avoids duplicating company details across sections.

date.description: ISO 8601 date when parseable; null if only a relative term was stated.
